roadmap.vision: Build the Gyro ecosystem from theory to runtime, application, project operations, and research platform.

phases.name: Theory
phases.status: v3.1 Release Review / Mathematical Formalization Study Active
in_progress: Minimal Formal Model v0 candidate selection
phases.next: Keep v3.1 release review separate from the next mathematical formalization research track

phases.name: Application
phases.status: Publication Milestones Completed / GyroAuth Formalization Study Active
completed: Criterion Integrity and Criterion Trajectory candidate structure
future_work: Criterion update audit and rollback model
Primary and applied paper next-version planning
phases.next: Keep the new concepts as research candidates until GyroAuth creates and reviews formal documents; do not add candidate fields to canonical schemas yet

phases.name: Ecosystem
phases.next: Reflect project updates and maintain weekly continuity

phases.name: Automation
items: Trajectory schema consistency checks after adoption
Criterion update history validation after adoption
phases.next: Keep GyroAuth candidate schemas unimplemented until formal adoption; then evaluate validation, audit, and rollback tooling

future_research_themes.name: Mathematical Foundations
future_research_themes.objective: Establish rigorous mathematical objects and relations for Gyro Logic concepts without changing the invariant Core.
completed_milestones: Initial candidate families for Structure, Slice, Stability, Difference, and Trajectory
Immediate failure warnings and non-decisions recorded
next_milestones: Decision on docs_index registration after content stabilization
topics: Relations among Structure, Slice, Stability, Difference, and Trajectory
Potential relationships with topology and topological data analysis
future_research_themes.boundary: This is a next-research-track artifact, not part of the v3.1 release candidate unless Gyro Logic explicitly decides otherwise. Candidate mathematical frameworks are not adopted foundations. Structure → Slice → Stability remains unchanged.

future_research_themes.name: Gyro Runtime Architecture
future_research_themes.status: Deferred while GyroOS v4.0 PoC is active
future_research_themes.objective: Clarify GyroOS as a runtime for executing and evaluating state-transition processes rather than as a replacement for a conventional operating-system kernel.
topics: Relationship with Linux, microkernels, workflow runtimes, and AI runtimes
Measurable safety, continuity, and operational advantages
Naming and positioning of GyroOS versus Gyro Runtime
future_research_themes.boundary: Do not introduce this theme into the current GyroOS v4.0 PoC unless the GyroOS project explicitly reopens it.

future_research_themes.name: Trajectory-Based Authentication and Criterion Integrity
future_research_themes.objective: Formalize what GyroAuth selects and how it evaluates both the authentication relation and the integrity of the changing criterion used for that selection.
future_research_themes.current_definition: Authentication = Stability-based Selection over State Convergence
next_milestones: Create Formal Security Model candidate document
Define admissible trajectory and viability-region boundaries
Define Criterion Integrity evaluation and bounded update process
Design criterion poisoning and baseline contamination simulations
Decide separation of primary-paper and applied-paper next versions
future_research_themes.boundary: All new concepts, fields, responses, and formulas remain candidates. GyroAuth must define and approve them before README, papers, canonical schemas, PoC contracts, or release candidates are changed. Gyro Logic Core and GyroOS Runtime definitions remain unchanged.

future_research_themes.name: Theory Identity
future_research_themes.objective: Clarify the primary object and explanatory scope of Gyro Logic.
future_research_themes.research_question: What is the primary mathematical and theoretical object of Gyro Logic?
future_research_themes.boundary: Candidate viewpoints are not new Core elements. This theme must distinguish the invariant Core from interpretations, derived concepts, temporal descriptions, and implementation models.
